title: The Prompting Paradigm
concept: Instead of fine-tuning model weights, prompting guides the frozen model to perform a task by describing it in the natural language input (context).

exercises.prompt: What is the main idea behind the prompting paradigm?

options.text: Keep the model frozen and guide it with text instructions.
Train the model's weights from scratch for every new prompt.
Delete the model's parameters to save storage space.

exercises.explain: Instead of changing weights, prompting guides a frozen model using natural language inputs (instructions).

cards.title: Brain Surgery vs. Conversation
cards.body: Imagine if you had to perform brain surgery on yourself every time you wanted to learn a new game rule. Sounds painful, right? In early AI, changing a model's behavior meant doing exactly that—updating millions of digital brain connections. But today, we just talk to it!

cards.title: What is Prompting?
cards.body: Prompting is the paradigm where we guide a pre-trained, frozen model using natural language instructions. Instead of changing the model's internal connections, we simply describe the task we want it to do in the input text.

cards.title: The Instant Chef
cards.body: Think of a frozen model as a world-class chef who already knows how to cook everything. Instead of sending them back to culinary school to learn a specific menu (fine-tuning), you simply hand them a recipe card (the prompt) explaining exactly what you want them to make today.

cards.title: Prompting in Action
cards.body: If you want an AI to write a polite email, you don't rewrite its brain. You write: "Translate this angry message into a polite email: 'Get this done now!'" The model reads the instructions and outputs: "Could you please prioritize this task?"

cards.title: The Weight Change Myth
cards.body: Some think that to make an AI do a new task, you must train it on thousands of new examples to change its weights. In reality, modern models are so smart that a clear instruction (a prompt) is often all they need to succeed instantly!

cards.title: Two Ways to Guide AI
cards.body: Let's look at the difference between updating the model's brain structure (Fine-Tuning) and simply giving it instructions (Prompting).

left.text: Updates model weights to learn a specific task. Slow and complex.

right.text: Uses natural language context on a frozen model. Instant and easy.

exercises.prompt: How do we tell a model what to do in the Prompting Paradigm?

options.text: By describing the task in natural language within the input.
By modifying the model's mathematical weights.
By training the model on thousands of new code files.
By deleting the model's memory of past tasks.

exercises.explain: Prompting is done by writing natural language descriptions (instructions) directly in the input context, leaving the model's weights completely untouched.

exercises.prompt: True or False: To use prompting, you must modify the internal weights of the AI model.
exercises.explain: Prompting does not require modifying model weights; the model remains frozen, and the instructions are passed in the input.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence to explain how prompting works.
exercises.sentence: Instead of changing weights, prompting guides a model whose weights remain ________.
exercises.explain: Prompting keeps the model's weights frozen (unchanged) and uses instructions in the input to direct its behavior.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of the Prompting Paradigm?

options.text: A text instruction: 'Translate the following English text to French: Hello'

exercises.explain: The Prompting prompt is an instruction written in English/natural language, whereas the code snippet is programming, and the weight array represents fine-tuning.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you need to quickly build an AI app that translates pirate slang. How does the Prompting Paradigm solve this without changing model weights?

options.text: We must rewrite the model's brain weights from scratch.
We guide a frozen model to perform a new task using natural language in the input.
We convert natural language prompts into computer code before the model can read it.
We must train the model for days on a supercomputer to change its behavior.

exercises.explain: Prompting lets us command a massive, frozen model to do a brand-new task instantly using only natural language instructions in the input!
